key_insight: A transfer-learning and hybrid Cross-Entropy–Lovász optimization upgrade makes BiSeNetV2 much more robust to reflective welding scenes without adding model complexity.
engineering_relevance: This offers a lightweight, real-time seam segmentation solution for construction welding robots operating under harsh illumination and specular metal reflections.
method: Fine-tuned a BiSeNetV2 backbone with transfer learning.
Used a hybrid Cross-Entropy + Lovász loss to improve boundary and overlap learning.
Kept FLOPs, parameter count, and inference speed unchanged versus the baseline.
evidence_signal: The proposed method reached 81.76% Joint IoU and 90.73% mIoU, improving Joint IoU by 22.36 points over the OHEM baseline and recovering 96.33% of severe zero-IoU failure cases.
watch_item: Performance gains are shown on reflective welding conditions, so deployment should still be validated across different metal types, lighting setups, and camera configurations.
paper_title: Enhanced Seam Segmentation for Automated Welding Robot in Construction Through Transfer Learning: Addressing Limitations of Bilateral Segmentation Network
